projects.title: Classic Wordmark Logo & Branding Assets for a SaaS company
projects.full_description:
# Logo & Brand Identity Design for SaaS Platform (NexBrix)

## Project Overview

NexBrix is a modern SaaS platform that helps businesses manage inventory, purchasing, suppliers, deliveries, workforce management, attendance, rostering, timesheets, reporting, and day-to-day operations from a single platform.

Our initial customers are cafés, restaurants, hospitality businesses, and multi-location operators, with future expansion into retail, warehousing, and service businesses.

We are looking for a branding designer to create a professional logo and visual identity that feels:

* Premium
* Sophisticated
* Modern
* Trustworthy
* Experienced
* Memorable

The brand should feel closer to Apple, Ramp, [login to view URL] and other modern software brands than traditional ERP or accounting software.

---

## Existing Direction

An initial concept has already been developed and will be shared with shortlisted candidates.

Current preferences:

* Wordmark-focused logo
* Clean and timeless typography
* Strong readability
* Elegant spacing and proportions
* Modern SaaS aesthetic
* Professional rather than playful

The final solution must work equally well on:

* Website
* Mobile app
* Social media
* Marketing materials
* Business documents

Brand name:

NexBrix

Preferred styling:

NexBrix

---

## What We Need

### 1. Logo Exploration

Provide at least 3 distinct logo concepts.

Examples may include:

* Pure wordmark
* Wordmark with subtle brand element
* Wordmark with supporting icon

We are open to creative direction.

---

### 2. Colour Exploration

We would like the designer to propose colour directions that align with the brand attributes above.

Please include multiple colour palette options and explain the thinking behind each direction.

---

### 3. Final Brand Assets

* Primary logo
* Secondary logo
* Monochrome versions
* Light and dark background versions
* App icon
* Favicon
* Social media icon

---

### 4. Mini Brand Guide

Include:

* Final colour palette
* Typography recommendations
* Logo clear-space rules
* Minimum sizing
* Usage examples

---

## Deliverables

Editable files:

* AI
* EPS
* PDF

Digital files:

* SVG
* PNG

---

## Revision Process

* Minimum 3 initial concepts
* Minimum 2 revision rounds
* Final source files transferred upon completion

---

## Timeline

Delivery required within 2-3 days.

---

## Proposal Requirements

Please include:

1. SaaS or technology branding examples.
2. Number of concepts included.
3. Number of revisions included.
4. Estimated delivery timeframe.
5. Confirmation that editable source files will be provided.

Shortlisted designers will receive the current concept and additional project information.

projects.title: Elite Rive Animator for Wellness iOS App

Rive animator for a premium consumer wellness iOS app
I'm looking for a skilled Rive animator to create a set of around 22 interactive animations for a premium consumer wellness app for iOS. The brand is calm, elegant, and refined — warm tones, soft greens and cream — so the motion should feel "alive but quiet," never flashy or cartoonish.
The work falls into two groups:

UI & feedback micro-interactions — a score-ring reveal and gentle breathing, card and badge reveals, a loading shimmer, tab-bar selection, progress/confidence bars, an XP float, and similar polished interface motion.
A few signature hero pieces — a mascot character with multiple states, a growing plant that develops through stages, and a couple of other key animated elements.

Deliverables:

Clean, optimized .riv files (ideally under ~300 KB for vector animations), each with a well-structured state machine, inputs, and properties.
Source project files and clear integration notes/documentation for our iOS developers.
For the hero pieces, recreating the supplied reference images as clean vectors before animating — so you'd handle both the vector artwork and the animation.

What I'll provide: a detailed animation brief, reference images, and full specs for every animation (exact palette, motion behaviour, timing, and the feel I'm after), so you'll have clear direction from day one.
Requirements: strong proficiency in Rive (state machines, inputs, optimized exports), experience animating app/UI interfaces, an eye for premium and restrained motion, and clear communication. Everything is documented and ready to share, so a motivated animator can start quickly. Please include relevant Rive work from your portfolio.

projects.full_description:
I need a clean, accurately proportioned 3D model of a step-cut kite-shaped diamond. The piece will be used purely for product visualization, so I’m only looking for a basic level of geometric detail—no microscopic facets or inclusions, just crisp edges, correct angles, and believable overall depth. 

I will handle materials, lighting, and final renders on my side; what I’m missing is the core geometry in an FBX file that imports smoothly into Blender or Maya without loose vertices, flipped normals, or non-manifold edges. If you model in Rhino, 3ds Max, or Cinema 4D that’s fine too, as long as the final export is a clean FBX.

Deliverables 
• Watertight step-cut kite diamond geometry 
• Single FBX file, scaled in millimeters 
• Pivot centered at the stone’s base for easy placement in my scene 

Once the mesh drops into my scene and passes a quick turntable check, the job is done. If that sounds straightforward to you, I’m ready to get started right away.

projects.title: Trading Card Purchasing Agent Bot
projects.full_description:
I want a fully-automated buying agent that can locate specific trading card products the moment they appear and complete checkout for me on specialized trading-card sites as well as other mainstream retailers I specify. The script should watch item pages or search results, detect restocks instantly, verify the price is within a threshold I set, and then execute the entire purchase flow—login, add to cart, shipping, and payment—without manual input.

Configurable fields (item URL or keyword, desired quantity, max price, billing/shipping profiles, scheduling) must sit in a simple JSON/YAML file or a small GUI so I can adjust targets quickly between drops. I need accurate status logging, basic error handling, and an optional Discord/webhook ping that confirms each successful order or flags a failure.

You’re free to pick the stack—Python with Selenium/Playwright or Node with Puppeteer are both fine so long as headless execution, proxy support, and captcha-solving hooks are possible. Clean, well-commented code and a short setup guide are the final deliverables; I should be able to run the bot on a Windows PC or Linux VPS with nothing more than the instructions you include.

Acceptance criteria:
• Monitors at least two named specialized trading-card sites plus one additional retailer I provide.
• Consistently checks out a low-stock test item in under 3 seconds from in-stock detection on a staging account.
• Allows me to swap login/payment profiles without code changes.
• Provides a concise log file and a webhook confirmation for each attempt.

If something here is unclear, feel free to flag it early so we can lock down the approach before you dive into coding.

projects.title: Real-Time AI Interview Avatar Developer / Engineer
projects.full_description:
I need an AI-driven avatar that can step in as the interviewer during live mock sessions on my platform, handling every aspect of a technical interview in real time. The avatar should greet the candidate, ask relevant questions, probe deeper when answers are vague, and keep track of time just like a seasoned human interviewer. After each session it should deliver an immediate summary and a structured assessment of the candidate’s performance.

The interviews will focus strictly on technical topics for now, so the underlying engine must be comfortable parsing and generating code questions and follow-ups in Python, JavaScript, and Java. I will supply the initial question bank plus evaluation rubrics; your role is to wire these into a conversational flow that feels natural and adaptive.

Core expectations:
• Real-time audio or video avatar capable of natural speech synthesis and lip sync 
• Dynamic question branching based on candidate responses 
• Live coding environment integration so the avatar can compile or run snippets when needed 
• Automatic scoring against my rubric and a brief, plain-language report at the end of each session 
• API hooks that let me trigger, monitor, and retrieve interview data from my existing platform

Acceptance criteria will be a demo interview where the avatar successfully evaluates a candidate in each of the three languages and returns the report through the API without manual intervention.

If future scope expands to behavioral or case study interviews I will look to build on the same framework, so clean, well-documented code is essential.

projects.title: Outlook 2010
projects.full_description:
I want a self-contained Python script that runs on my Windows 10 PC (where Outlook 2010 is already installed and configured). When I launch the script it should:

1. Open Outlook, point straight to the Sent Items folder, grab the most recent message and place it in Forward mode. 
2. Strip “FW:” from the subject line. 
3. Remove the automatically generated header lines (“From:”, “Sent:”, “To:”, “Subject:”) that appear at the top of the body after a forward—everything else in the body stays exactly as is.

I will consider the job complete when I can run the script, watch Outlook send batches of 250 BCC recipients from my Sent Items thread, and see [login to view URL] steadily decrease until empty, with the subject and body edits applied exactly as specified.

projects.full_description:
I am developing a range of sous vide main-course recipes for hospital and home-care patients in India and I need a food-tech minded chef or nutritionist to help me finalise them.

Scope 
• All dishes must be cooked entirely (or at least finish-cooked) sous vide. 
• Cuisine style: fusion—Indian flavours or ingredients paired with complementary international techniques or seasonings. 
• Dietary targets: low-sodium and diabetic-friendly are non-negotiable. In addition, I want clearly labelled variants that hit different macronutrient goals: high-protein recovery meals, low-protein renal-safe versions, low-carb portions, and complex-carb-focused options for sustained energy. 
• Ingredients must be readily sourced in India and cost-effective for institutional kitchens.

Deliverables 
1. Ten complete recipes (PDF or DOCX) with: 
 – exact ingredient weights, sous vide temperature & time, and finishing instructions 
 – plating/portion notes for a 300 kcal and a 450 kcal serving 
 – full nutrition panel per serving and per 100 g (Calories, Protein, Carbs—simple vs complex, Fat, Sodium, Fibre, Net Carbs, GI estimate) 
2. A master ingredient matrix so hospitals can bulk-order efficiently 
3. Food safety/HACCP notes specific to sous vide holding and regeneration in Indian conditions

Acceptance Criteria 
• Every recipe passes <500 mg sodium per 300 kcal serving and maintains ≤12 g simple sugars. 
• Temperature and time tables follow USDA/WHO safety guidelines for the stated proteins. 
• Flavour profiles reflect at least one recognisable Indian spice or regional element while offering a global twist.

If you have prior experience designing clinical or diet-specific menus, especially with immersion circulators (Anova, Polyscience, etc.) and nutrition-analysis software such as Cronometer or NutriBase, your insight will be invaluable. I look forward to seeing how you can help make healthy, exciting sous vide meals accessible to patients across India.

My budget for the same is 1000 INR only

projects.title: Power BI Report Resolution for Databricks
projects.full_description:
Earlier:
Dataverse only → Power BI semantic model → Paginated report
This worked because all required columns were coming from one supported model/source.
Now:
Dataverse + Azure Databricks columns → same Power BI semantic model → Paginated report
Paginated report is failing/blank because the semantic model now has a mixed source model, and the Databricks master columns may not be resolving properly through the semantic model when paginated report queries it.
Most likely causes:
The Databricks columns are not imported/refreshed properly in the semantic model
Power BI visual may show them due to local/desktop cache or DirectQuery behavior.
Paginated report reads the published semantic model, not your desktop file.
Relationship issue between Dataverse table and Databricks master table
If relationship is inactive, many-to-many, wrong key, or filter direction issue, paginated report DAX may return blank.
Paginated report query is still based on old Dataverse-only fields
After adding Databricks columns, the dataset query in Report Builder may need to be refreshed/rebuilt.
Composite model / DirectQuery issue
If Dataverse is Import and Databricks is DirectQuery, paginated report may behave differently while querying the semantic model.
Credentials/gateway issue for Databricks in Power BI Service
Dataset may publish, but service-side refresh/query may fail for Databricks credentials.

All work will be done during a mutually agreed maintenance window; I can supply VPN or direct-desktop access as required. If you are fluent with Windows Server 2016, Windows 11, Active Directory, and event-log forensics, this should be a concise engagement.
